name: Household Crowding / Housing Density
definition: Household crowding and housing density are exposure context variables relevant to infectious disease transmission patterns and broader health outcomes in population studies. GENARCH includes this category to support educational community and infectious susceptibility narratives, without individual-level inference.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Crowding can increase exposure opportunities for respiratory pathogens; effects depend on ventilation, resources, and community factors.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Crowding affects exposure opportunities across ages; vulnerability differs by immune status and access to care.

references.title: Housing conditions and respiratory infection risk: overview (review)
